title: Production Edge Agentic AI for Mining Safety
category: Agentic AI + Edge AI + Computer Vision
one_liner: Designed real-time on-device agentic AI systems for safety-critical video analytics on NVIDIA Jetson AGX.
problem: Mining and industrial environments require continuous safety monitoring, but manual monitoring is expensive, slow, and error-prone.
solution: Built multimodal edge AI pipelines combining perception, reasoning, and action loops for real-time video analytics under strict latency, compute, power, and reliability constraints.
technical_details: Computer vision pipelines for hazard detection and workflow automation
Edge-aware optimization for latency, memory, and throughput
Client-facing delivery with stakeholder demos and implementation roadmaps
suggested_visuals: Pipeline flow: video stream → perception → reasoning → action
recruiter_angle: Shows rare combination of agentic AI, computer vision, edge deployment, and production constraints.

title: Video-RAG for Continuous Video Search
one_liner: Architected retrieval-oriented video understanding pipelines for natural-language search over live and long-horizon video streams.
problem: Long video streams are difficult to search, reason over, and summarize using traditional indexing approaches.
solution: Built a Video-RAG pipeline using compressed spatio-temporal embeddings, CLIP/VLM-derived representations, semantic text embeddings, multimodal indexing, and LLM reasoning.
impact: Sub-second search latency over multi-day indexed video
Contextual querying and decision support over live video streams
technical_details: TensorRT / FP16 optimization on Jetson AGX Orin
recruiter_angle: Strong differentiator for GenAI, RAG, multimodal AI, and applied research roles.

title: Enterprise AI for Sprint Planning and Resource Allocation
one_liner: Developed an agentic planning workflow to support prioritization, sequencing, trade-off analysis, and resource allocation.
problem: Product, engineering, and operations teams need better visibility into dependencies, constraints, and competing priorities during planning.
solution: Formalized product definitions, feature requirements, sprint goals, and workflow logic into structured context for AI-assisted decision support.
impact: Improved visibility into dependencies and constraints
Framed as a human-AI collaboration system for workflow redesign
technical_details: Research framing around organizational AI adoption
recruiter_angle: Best case study for AI consulting, strategy, forward-deployed AI, and product-facing AI roles.

title: High-Performance Inference and MLOps for Real-Time AI
one_liner: Built GPU-accelerated inference and deployment pipelines that improved throughput, latency, reproducibility, and validation.
problem: Production AI workloads need predictable latency, efficient memory usage, reproducible deployment, and continuous validation.
solution: Engineered real-time and batch inference pipelines using ONNX, TensorRT, CUDA, GStreamer, MLflow, Docker, AWS, CI/CD, and edge-device runners.
recruiter_angle: Targets ML systems, infra, edge AI, and performance engineering recruiters.

title: Real-Time Infrastructure Defect Detection
one_liner: Built real-time anomaly detection and inspection systems integrating live drone feeds, ML predictions, and analytics dashboards.
problem: Infrastructure inspection workflows are slow and costly when dependent on manual review.
solution: Developed ensemble ML models and real-time analytics pipelines for structural defect classification.
recruiter_angle: Proves applied ML product delivery before JSquared.

title: LLM-Based Pull Request Review Assistant
one_liner: Built an LLM-based pull request review system to automate code analysis and improve engineering efficiency.
problem: Code review can be slow, inconsistent, and difficult to scale across fast-moving AI development teams.
solution: Implemented an LLM-driven review workflow to analyze PRs, surface issues, and support engineering quality.
impact: Supported faster applied AI development workflows
recruiter_angle: Good supporting project for AI software engineer and internal tools roles.

title: Data Collection System — Edge-to-Cloud File Sync Sidecar
one_liner: Designed and built a production edge sidecar (Python) that syncs field-device files to AWS S3 using certificate-based IoT authentication, with a crash-safe multi-process architecture and full supply-chain-secured CI/CD.
problem: Unattended edge devices in industrial environments must reliably upload locally produced files to the cloud for months at a time — surviving crashes, power loss, and network outages without ever losing or double-uploading data, and without storing any static cloud credentials on the device.
solution: Built a fork-based supervisor process that isolates three worker processes (file discovery, upload, and cleanup) communicating only through a shared SQLite ledger with atomic, crash-safe state transitions. Files are uploaded to AWS S3 using short-lived credentials obtained via AWS IoT X.509 certificate authentication, confirmed with a backend API, and deleted only after a retention-gated, confirmed sync.
impact: 235 passing tests across unit, container, and live tiers
0 static AWS keys on any edge device
technical_details: Fork-safe multi-process architecture: a SystemOrchestrator supervises isolated Walker/Uploader/Cleaner children with restart-storm throttling and a staged shutdown ladder
Zero-ambient-credentials security model: every S3 client requires explicit IoT-issued temporary credentials via an X.509 → role-alias → STS exchange
Crash-safe file lifecycle with an atomic compare-and-swap claim, a stuck-upload watchdog, and an append-only deletion/registration audit ledger
Two-stage Docker build that compiles a patched SQLite (SHA3-256 pinned) to fix a WAL-checkpoint correctness bug, enforced by a boot-time and build-time safety gate
Jenkins CI/CD with SonarQube quality gates, cosign image signing, and SPDX SBOM generation/attestation shipped to Harbor and AWS ECR
Reusable path-traversal-safe zip extraction utility closing a zip-slip vulnerability in the credential-provisioning flow
suggested_visuals: File lifecycle state machine (pending → uploading → synced/failed)
Zero-credential IoT → STS credential exchange flow
recruiter_angle: Demonstrates production systems, security, and edge/IoT engineering depth beyond model work — crash-safe distributed process design and end-to-end supply-chain-secured delivery, owned solo.

title: Visitor Log — Multi-Site Visitor Management Platform
one_liner: Architected and built end to end a multi-site visitor-management platform with security-classified approval routing, signed QR badge credentials, and self-service kiosks on a zero-trust FastAPI + Next.js + PostgreSQL stack.
problem: A multi-site organization needed to replace manual, ad-hoc visitor tracking with a governed workflow that routes each visit through the correct security approval based on classification, protects sensitive PII, and produces a complete, auditable record of every visit and system access.
solution: Designed a 3-tier system (Cloudflare Access → Next.js BFF → FastAPI API → PostgreSQL) that routes visit requests to the right approver by security classification, collects and encrypts only the PII each classification requires, and issues signed, site-scoped QR credentials that unattended kiosks use for check-in, badge printing, and checkout — all behind zero-trust, per-application edge authentication.
technical_details: Zero-trust, per-area JWT audience scoping so tokens from one Cloudflare Access surface cannot be replayed on another; removed an unsafe shared-cookie design
Custom signed QR credential format (VLQ1) with per-site key rotation/revocation that retires compromised keys without invalidating previously issued credentials
Field-level PII encryption at rest, gated by visit classification, with masked display everywhere policy does not require it
Layered FastAPI service (routers → services → repositories → models) behind a mandatory internal service token, failing closed on missing production config
Carried a live PostgreSQL schema through two breaking domain-model changes across 18 linear Alembic migrations with no data loss
Jenkins CI/CD running parallel Dockerized backend (pytest) and frontend (Vitest + tsc + ESLint) stages, promoting production images to Harbor on merge
suggested_visuals: 3-tier architecture: Cloudflare Access → Next.js BFF → FastAPI → PostgreSQL
Signed QR credential issuance + kiosk check-in sequence
recruiter_angle: Strong end-to-end full-stack and security case study — solo-architected zero-trust platform with signed credentials, encrypted PII, and CI/CD from scratch, delivered v1.0.0 in 29 days.
